COS10004 Computer Systems
               Lecture 4.3:  Let‚Äôs build a stack!







Dr Chris McCarthy

             STACKS
             >‚ÄØ  Random access memory requires knowing the address of
                 every byte/word you want to access
             >‚ÄØ  Hardware stacks created out of dedicated shift registers
             >‚ÄØ  So let‚Äôs build one!


















2

LET'S BUILD A STACK...
>‚ÄØ  Start with a bi-directional shift register...
>‚ÄØ  1. Start off with 4 D flip-flops:























                                                                                 3

2. ADD THE STANDARD SIGNALS
>‚ÄØ Clock



























                                                               4

>‚ÄØ  CLR (resets flip flops when up in Logisim)



























                                                                              5

ADD THE SERIAL INPUT (D)
>‚ÄØ Data is the serial input (SI)



























                                                                         6

CONNECT EACH Q OUTPUT TO THE NEXT DATA INPUT






























                                                          7

ADD SOME LEDS TO SEE THE PARALLEL OUTPUT / REGISTER
STATE
>‚ÄØ We can now modulate the Data input and see the on state
   propagate through the register (left to right).

























                                                                     8

THIS CIRCUIT WILL DO MOST THINGS WE NEED:
>‚ÄØ To get serial out, record the state of the right-most LED.
>‚ÄØ To get parallel out, feed each LED into a register (latch)
   and stop the clock when the conversion is complete.
>‚ÄØ To do parallel input, OR each D input with the state of a
   register and start the clock.










          but it won't go backwards...
                                                                           9

  HOW DO WE MAKE IT GO BACKWARDS?

>‚ÄØ Wire-up the cascade backwards
>‚ÄØ Data goes in the far end, each Q outputs to the D input of
   the previous Flip-Flop.





















                                                                       10

   HOW DO WE MAKE THE DIRECTION SELECTABLE?
   >‚ÄØ  Remember those controlled logic gates in Week 2 ?







Select this
AND gate
 (Ci low)

Select this
 OR gate
 (Ci high)



                                                                                11

HOW DO WE MAKE THE DIRECTION SELECTABLE?
>‚ÄØ More specifically, we need to determine from which
   direction each Flip Flop will receive its input.
























                                                                      12

HOW DO WE MAKE THE DIRECTION SELECTABLE?
>‚ÄØ A controlled gate (remember Week 2!).


























                                                                13

      PROGRAMMABLE WIRING

      >‚ÄØ  We can use one of these circuits for each input to a Flip-
          Flop, and use a common control signal to determine
          direction.






A, B'     Output
1         A
0         B









                                                                                14

       SELECTING THE OUTPUT WITH GATES
       >‚ÄØ  We also need to determine which direction output from a
           Flip Flop flows!
       >‚ÄØ  This circuit has a common input, and selectable output.
       >‚ÄØ  We can use one of these for each output from a Flip-Flop,
           and use a common control signal to determine direction.

A, B'     Input

1         A
0         B








                                                                                 15

LET'S PUT THIS ALL TOGETHER
 (JUST 2 FFS TO START WITH)




























                                                              16

ADD THE GATES FOR SETTING INPUT






























                                                            17

SELECT INPUT






























                                                                18

               ADD THE OUTPUT DIRECTION SELECTION
               >‚ÄØ  R circuit enabled (red)




                                       in                                 out

                                       in                                 out


Input             D
 Q             D










                                                                                             19

               OTHER DIRECTION SELECTED?







                                        in                                   out

                                        in                                   out


Input             D
 Q             D










                                                                                                 20

             WHAT COULD POSSIBLY GO WRONG?
             >‚ÄØ  Can't short the outputs of two gates together.
             >‚ÄØ  Have to Add with an OR gate.



                                   in                             out

                                   in                             out

Conflict












                                                                                    21

         THE FIX
         >‚ÄØ  The OR gates combine the outputs of the controlling AND
             gates and pass through the signal from whichever one is
             enabled... to the D inputs on each Flip-Flop.



                                 in       ff 1  ff 2                out

                                 in                                 out


 Generally need
about 3 gates per
  flip-flop per bit





                                                                                       22

NEED MORE DEPTH?
>‚ÄØ This is a 4-deep shift register (
   http://www.ee.usyd.edu.au/tutorials/digital_tutorial/part2/
   register06.html).
>‚ÄØ As you can see, there are alternative ways of wiring it up,
   with different logic gates.

















                                                                             23

TO MAKE A STACK...

>‚ÄØ  So far we have made a 2-stage bi-directional 1-bit shift
    register.
>‚ÄØ  To make a proper stack:
     1.‚ÄØ  Add depth (flip-flops with associated control logic)
     2.‚ÄØ  Add width (bits) in parallel (common clock, control
          signals).
          ‚Ä¢‚ÄØ  identical shift-registers - one for each bit.










                                                                              24

SUMMARY

>‚ÄØ  Hardware stacks formed using banks of shift registers
>‚ÄØ  Simple input selective circuits allow direction selectability:
     ‚Äì‚ÄØ Programmable gates !
>‚ÄØ  To make a proper stack:
     1.‚ÄØ  Add depth (flip-flops with associated control logic)
     2.‚ÄØ  Add width (bits) in parallel (common clock, control
          signals).
         ‚Ä¢‚ÄØ   identical shift-registers - one for each bit.






                                                                             25
